title: Are repeated pregnancy ultrasounds harmful?
excerpt: Ultrasound uses sound waves, not X-rays; stick to medically indicated scans — Long Châu, FDA.
content:
## How ultrasound works

High-frequency sound imaging—**no ionizing radiation** like CT.

## Safety

When performed by trained staff for **clinical indications**, ultrasound is considered safe. Avoid unnecessary prolonged “keepsake” sessions.

## Typical schedule

- Dating and fetal heart confirmation.
- Anatomy screening (timing per local protocol).
- Growth, placenta, fluid checks when indicated.

## Bottom line

Trust your obstetric plan; do not self-order excessive scans from anxiety.
